zh.León, León, León, Castilla y León, Espan: 西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安利安利安
zh.lifeSummary: 當Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera於大約1085年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安利安利安時，他的父親Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera107歲，母親María Froilaz20歲。他於1020年與Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava在葡萄牙可布拉可布拉結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世在葡萄牙可布拉。

zh-hans.León, León, León, Castilla y León, Espan: 西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安利安利安
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera于大约1085年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安利安利安利安时，他的父亲Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera107岁，母亲María Froilaz20岁。他于1020年与Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava在葡萄牙可布拉可布拉结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世在葡萄牙可布拉。

nameMeanings.name: Sanchez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro, Ramon, Raul, Roberto.
Spanish (Sánchez): patronymic from the personal name Sancho . In the US, this surname is also found among Native Americans (mainly Pueblos). Compare De Sanchez .

variants.name: Saez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Diego, Jorge, Juan, Carlos, Enrique, Miguel, Pedro, Rafael, Guillermo, Jaime.
Spanish (Sáez): variant of Sánchez (see Sanchez ).

variants.name: Sancho
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Elena, Carlos, Fernando, Jacinto, Mario, Agustin, Ana, Dulce, Emilio, Enrique, Eugenio.

Spanish and Portuguese: from Sancho, a popular medieval personal name, which is probably from Latin Sanc(t)ius, a derivative of sanctus ‘holy’. The personal name was borne by a 9th-century Christian martyr of Cordova.

variants.name: Saenz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Raul, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Mario, Francisco, Pedro, Ricardo, Alfredo.
Spanish (Sáenz): variant of Sánchez (see Sanchez ).

variants.name: Sanz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Ricardo, Juan, Luis, Enrique, Jaime, Armando, Ernesto, Francisco, Manuel, Miguel.
German: from a short form, Sando, of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with sand ‘true’.
German: habitational name from a place called Sanz in Mecklenburg-West Pomerania.

nameMeanings.name: El
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Finnish Arto, Kauko, Reino, Seppo.  Scandinavian Jarl.  Hungarian Arpad, Atle, Csaba.

Finnish: ornamental name from elo ‘life, harvest’;‘grain’. It was adopted by many Finns during the name conversionmovement in the 19th and early 20th centuries, especially in southernand western parts of the country.
Hungarian(Élő): from a pet form of Éliás (see Elias ).

variants.name: Elias
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Miguel, Carlos, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Mario, Jorge, Ramon, Francisco, Fernando, Jesus.

Spanish (Elías); French (also Élias); Greek, Catalan, Portuguese, English, Welsh, German, Dutch, Breton, Assyrian/Chaldean, and Jewish; Hungarian (Éliás); Czech (Eliáš); Slovak (Eliáš and Eliaš): from a personal name, taken from the New Testament Greek form (Ēlias) of Hebrew Eliyahu ‘Jehovah is God’ (Anglicized as Elijah in the Old Testament of the King James Bible). This name was borne by a Biblical prophet, but its popularity among Christians in the Middle Ages was largely a result of its adoption by various early saints, including a 7th-century bishop of Syracuse and a 9th-century Spanish martyr. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Eliya or Elia , Croatian and Slovenian Ilijaš or Iljaš. In medieval England the name generally took the form Ellis but in the 18th and 19th centuries Welsh Nonconformists adopted the form Elias as a patronymic.

nameMeanings.name: Velloso
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: variant of   Veloso  .

variants.name: Veloso
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Roberto, Juan, Alfredo, Carlos, Fernando, Luis, Nicanor, Abilio, Agustina, Aida.  Portuguese Joaquim.
Portuguese and Galician:
nickname for a hirsute individual, from veloso ‘hairy’ (from Latin villosus).

nameMeanings.name: Rodrigo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Visigothic: composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame’ + rīc ‘power’. It was the name of the last king of the Visigoths, defeated by the Moors in 711 .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.description: Afonso inherits the County of Portugal, a province of Castile.
The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera was born about 1085, in León, León, León, Castilla y León, Spain, his father, Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera, was 107 and his mother, María Froilaz, was 20. He married Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava in 1020, in Coimbra, Coimbra, Portugal. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in Coimbra, Portugal.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera est né environ 1085, à León, León, León, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera, avait 107 ans et sa mère, María Froilaz, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava en 1020, à Coimbra, Coimbra, Portugal. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé à Coimbra, Portugal.

de.lifeSummary: Als Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera um 1085 in León, León, León, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera 107 und seine Mutter María Froilaz 20. Er hat 1020 in Coimbra, Coimbra, Portugal Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb in Coimbra, Portugal.

Lui sposato Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava nel 1020, nel Coimbra, Coimbra, Portogallo. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Coimbra, Portogallo.

ja.lifeSummary: Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Riberaが約1085年にスペインカスティラ イ レoンレoンレoンレoンで生まれた時，父Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Riberaは107歳，母María Froilazは20歳であった。1020年にポルトガルコインブラコインブラでElvira Ponce de Minerva y Travaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼はポルトガルコインブラで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera(이)가 대략 1085년 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 레o 레o 레o에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera(은)는 107세였고, 그의 어머니 María Froilaz(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1020년 포르투갈 코임브라 코임브라에서 Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 포르투갈 코임브라에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Don Rodrigo de Sanchez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera родился около 1085 г. в местности леoн, леoн, леoн, кастилла и леoн, Испания, его отцу по имени Sancho Ramirez El Velloso Señor de Cabrera y de Ribera было 107 лет, а матери, которую звали María Froilaz, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Elvira Ponce de Minerva y Trava в 1020 г. в местности коимбра, коимбра, Португалия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь.
